title: Metal hybrid bead cores for tires
abstract: A vehicle tire having two beads intended to come into contact with a wheel rim and a carcass reinforcement anchored by a bead core located in each bead, wherein the bead core may include a plurality of separate spirally-wound reinforcements that are placed adjacent to one another in a substantially axial arrangement. The innermost of the spirally-wound reinforcements that are made of wire, that is the one farthest from the reinforcement turn-up, is formed of a first metal wire (7i) and there is a second metal wire (7o) forming a second of the reinforcements. The first metal wire may be characterized as having a greater elongation at break than the other metal wires in the bead core, typically at least 40% greater or alternatively at least 50% or at least 125% greater. Optionally wires of other metals or nonmetals may be useful for forming others of the plurality of spirally-wound reinforcements.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. A tire for a vehicle, comprising: two beads intended to come into contact with a wheel rim, a bead core positioned in each of the beads, a carcass reinforcement wrapped around the bead cores from an innermost side of the bead core to an outermost side of the bead core in order to form a reinforcement turn-up on the outermost side of the bead core, the bead core comprising a plurality of separate substantially axially adjacent spirally- wound reinforcements;; a first metal wire positioned as an innermost of the plurality of spirally-wound reinforcements that are formed of metal wires, to form the innermost side of the bead core; a second metal wire forming a second reinforcement of the plurality of spirally-wound reinforcements, wherein the first metal wire has an elongation at break at least 40% greater than an elongation at break of the second metal wire. 2. The tire of claim 1, wherein the metal wire forming the innermost of the spirally- wound reinforcements has an elongation at break at least 50% greater than an elongation at break of the second metal wire. 3. The tire of claim 1 or 2, wherein the metal wire forming the innermost of the spirally- wound reinforcements has an elongation at break at least 125% greater than an elongation at break of the second metal wire. 4. The tire of any of the preceding claims, further comprising: a third metal wire forming a third reinforcement of the plurality of spirally-wound reinforcements, wherein the second metal wire elongation at break is lower than the third metal wire's elongation at break. 5. The tire of claim 4, wherein the third metal wire is a single filament carbon steel wire. 6. The tire of any of the preceding claims, wherein the first and second metal wires are single filament carbon steel wires. 7. The tire of any of the preceding claims, further comprising: a nonmetallic wire forming at least one of the plurality of spirally- wound reinforcements. 8. The tire of claim 7, wherein the nonmetallic wire is a carbon fiber composite wire. 9. The tire of claim 7 or 8, wherein the nonmetallic wire forms the innermost of the plurality of the spirally- wound reinforcements. 10. The tire of claim 7 or 8, wherein the nonmetallic wire forms the outermost of the plurality of the spirally-wound reinforcements, the outermost being farthest from the interior of the tire. 11. The tire of any of the preceding claims, wherein a shape of the wires forming the plurality of spirally- wound reinforcements is selected from circular, oval, polygonal, hexagonal, rectangular or square. 12. The tire of any of the preceding claims, wherein dimensions of the wires forming the plurality of spirally- wound reinforcements are identical. 13. The tire of any of the preceding claims, wherein the wires forming the plurality of spirally- wound reinforcements are between 0.7 mm and 5 mm wide. 14. The tire of any of the preceding claims, wherein the wires forming the plurality of spirally- wound reinforcements are between 2 mm and 3 mm wide. 15. The tire of any of the preceding claims, wherein the first metal wire forms the innermost of all of the plurality of spirally-wound reinforcements. 16. The tire of any of the preceding claims, wherein the tire is a heavy vehicle tire. 

The tire of claim 15, wherein the tire is a freight truck tire.
